verses.text: Against Pharaoh, 'the great dragon' in his rivers, who boasts, 'My river is mine own, and I have made it for myself.' Proud self-sufficiency. To claim one's blessings as self-made denies the Giver.
Against Pharaoh, 'the great dragon' in his rivers, who boasts, 'My river is mine own, and I have made it for myself.' Proud self-sufficiency. To claim one's blessings as self-made denies the Giver.
Against Pharaoh, 'the great dragon' in his rivers, who boasts, 'My river is mine own, and I have made it for myself.' Proud self-sufficiency. To claim one's blessings as self-made denies the Giver.
God will put hooks in his jaws and fling him into the wilderness for the beasts. The monster hauled out. The mightiest is helpless in the hand of God.
God will put hooks in his jaws and fling him into the wilderness for the beasts. The monster hauled out. The mightiest is helpless in the hand of God.
Egypt was 'a staff of reed' to Israel, splintering when leaned on. False support. Trust in worldly powers wounds those who lean on it.
Egypt was 'a staff of reed' to Israel, splintering when leaned on. False support. Trust in worldly powers wounds those who lean on it.
God will bring a sword and make Egypt desolate, because Pharaoh said, 'The river is mine.' Pride judged. The boast of ownership brings the loss of all.
God will bring a sword and make Egypt desolate, because Pharaoh said, 'The river is mine.' Pride judged. The boast of ownership brings the loss of all.
He will lay Egypt waste 'from Migdol to Syene,' desolate forty years, and scatter the Egyptians. Measured judgment. God sets even the term of a nation's chastening.
He will lay Egypt waste 'from Migdol to Syene,' desolate forty years, and scatter the Egyptians. Measured judgment. God sets even the term of a nation's chastening.
He will lay Egypt waste 'from Migdol to Syene,' desolate forty years, and scatter the Egyptians. Measured judgment. God sets even the term of a nation's chastening.
After forty years He will gather Egypt again, but as 'a base kingdom,' no longer Israel's confidence. Humbled, not destroyed. God breaks the idol His people leaned on.
After forty years He will gather Egypt again, but as 'a base kingdom,' no longer Israel's confidence. Humbled, not destroyed. God breaks the idol His people leaned on.
After forty years He will gather Egypt again, but as 'a base kingdom,' no longer Israel's confidence. Humbled, not destroyed. God breaks the idol His people leaned on.
After forty years He will gather Egypt again, but as 'a base kingdom,' no longer Israel's confidence. Humbled, not destroyed. God breaks the idol His people leaned on.
Nebuchadnezzar's army served hard against Tyre 'yet had... no wages' for the labour. Toil unrewarded. Even God's instrument is repaid by His hand, not man's.
Nebuchadnezzar's army served hard against Tyre 'yet had... no wages' for the labour. Toil unrewarded. Even God's instrument is repaid by His hand, not man's.
So God gives Egypt to Babylon as his wages; 'in that day will I cause the horn of... Israel to bud.' Recompense and hope. The same providence judges Egypt and revives Israel.
